notes:
Production durability polish
- Realtime transcription failures now surface fallback mode immediately while reconnect attempts continue in the background.
- Meeting decisions, action items, open questions, and rename/export snapshots stay synchronized more reliably across durable storage and downstream evidence feeds.
- Knowledge enrichment now preserves the intended trust hierarchy by allowing clear private/internal evidence to outrank weaker public-reference disagreement, with new regressions covering the rule.

release_notes.notes:
Production durability polish
- Realtime transcription failures now surface fallback mode immediately while reconnect attempts continue in the background.
- Meeting decisions, action items, open questions, and rename/export snapshots stay synchronized more reliably across durable storage and downstream evidence feeds.
- Knowledge enrichment now preserves the intended trust hierarchy by allowing clear private/internal evidence to outrank weaker public-reference disagreement, with new regressions covering the rule.

Settings clarity and release history
- Settings now shows useful release TLDR notes all the time, with the latest release visible first and earlier releases available in a bounded scrolling history.
- Enrichment-source examples and active connection rows are much denser, so source setup and health are easier to scan without losing important status detail.
- Recent concept-memory display filters more low-signal transcript residue, reducing junk concepts that should not be treated as durable knowledge.

Recurring concept cleanup
- Automatic recurring-concept enrichment no longer promotes ambiguous single-token transcript leftovers into durable memory on its own.
- Suppressed Context Library rows stay hidden from active listings while still remaining available to audit-oriented flows.
- Regression coverage was added for observed junk seeds such as API, Hey, MIT, Nice, Very, and XYZ.

Grounded meeting-fact precision
- Meeting fact extraction keeps structured accepted context line-oriented instead of flattening separate evidence lines into one blob.
- Durable knowledge explanations no longer leak into customer or organization facts as if they were meeting participants.
- Markdown regeneration keeps accepted context synchronized without recreating flattened duplicate context blocks.

Priority enrichment source planning
- Barn Owl gained clearer product and verification guidance for priority enrichment sources, including how higher-value context should be assembled and evaluated.
- CLI and Codex-facing documentation were tightened so enrichment setup, QA, and handoff flows are easier to execute consistently.
- Focused tests and source-handoff verification were expanded around the release workflow.

Transcript durability and context grounding
- Realtime hinting, transcript context handling, and meeting-fact extraction were strengthened so trusted context stays better aligned with final notes and local artifacts.
- Persistence and local library update paths were tightened to keep regenerated content synchronized across stored meeting outputs.
- The app, CLI, and test coverage expanded around repair, enrichment, and production-readiness workflows.

Meeting evidence export and control surface
- Barn Owl added a richer control/export surface for meeting evidence, allowing downstream consumers and operator workflows to inspect more structured recording state.
- Settings, recorder, and menu-bar experiences were broadened alongside control-command and persistence support.
- The release included substantial architecture docs and automated coverage for evidence export, enrichment operations, and local durability.

Packaging hardening
- Packaging scripts were tightened for Barn Owl app distribution and source handoff generation.
- Release artifact creation was made more resilient so later update-feed work had a cleaner base.
